failure_modes.text: Optimization drifts into proxy worship when a measurable indicator or elegant model output replaces the real outcome.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when the proxy becomes the target: Danger when teams optimize a measurable indicator, local process metric, or elegant model output that no longer tracks the real outcome they claim to care about.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the objective function: verify that the metric being optimized is still a faithful proxy for the real outcome, and sunset it if the gap has grown.

failure_modes.text: Teams boil the ocean when they optimize for completeness instead of filtering toward the core leverage points.
failure_modes.source_quote: Optimization necessitates filtering out the nonessential to focus on the core leverage points.
failure_modes.mitigation: Apply the Pareto filter early: identify the 2-3 levers that drive most of the outcome and defer completeness until the core leverage is captured.

failure_modes.text: A favorite framework can become a shackle when it is reused on a problem whose assumptions no longer fit.
failure_modes.source_quote: Applying a favorite or familiar mental model ... to every problem, even novel ones, simply because it's readily available.
failure_modes.mitigation: Mandate a periodic framework rotation: after two cycles with the same optimization model, force a pass with a structurally different approach to detect assumption drift.

premortem_questions.text: What are we actually maximizing or minimizing?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Most useful when the team can say what it is actually maximizing or minimizing and can distinguish the true target from a convenient proxy.

premortem_questions.text: What are we sacrificing, what are we protecting, and which metric wins when goals conflict?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Best when trade-offs must be made explicit: Best when a recommendation should state what is being sacrificed, what is being protected, and which metric wins when goals conflict.

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Ask: "Is the worst case bad enough?"

heuristics.text: Name the objective function before debating options so the comparison is anchored to the real target.
heuristics.source_quote: Optimization is intrinsically linked to defining clear goals.

heuristics.text: Make constraints operational instead of decorative so the recommendation fits actual limits on time, capital, risk, or bottlenecks.
heuristics.source_quote: Works when constraints are real rather than decorative.

heuristics.text: Reduce extraneous complexity so effort concentrates on leverage points rather than on low-value completeness.
heuristics.source_quote: Optimization necessitates filtering out the nonessential to focus on the core leverage points.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on objective clarity, proxy-risk discipline, and constraint-aware trade-off work.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor optimization rhetoric that widened into general productivity language beyond the raw source's stronger leverage and proxy-risk material.
open_questions: The second failure mode compresses several anti-completeness passages so it remains reusable across strategy and analysis settings.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking, cognitive load theory, and leverage-oriented models for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on confirmation-bias-adjacent framework lock and on uncertainty-heavy contexts that resist strict maximization.
structured_tensions: The source has real tension material around proxy targets and uncertainty, but it does not present a clean explicit structured tension block in current-vocabulary form.
